summary: Crystal‑clear audience tape of a high‑energy Radio City show with standout acoustic set and killer moments like Mexicali Blues, Casey Jones and China Doll
review: The recording is an audience (AUD) capture that many fans describe as “hissless,” “crystal clear” and among the best of the 1980 series, despite a slight pitch fastness. Musically the show shines with an exceptional acoustic set and memorable electric highlights – Feel Like a Stranger, Mexicali Blues, Big Railroad Blues, Music Never Stopped, Samson & Delilah, Casey Jones and a soaring China Doll finale – though Sugar Magnolia is noted as only so‑so.

ai_rating.rationale: Consistently high star ratings (mostly 5) and enthusiastic language point to an excellent audience recording and a very good performance; weighted scoring yields ~4.2

recording_quality.technical_notes: Listeners praise crystal‑clear, hissless sound with strong bass response; minor issues mentioned are a slight fast pitch (-1%) and the tape taking a moment to settle early in the set.

show_quality.setlist_flow: The acoustic set opens the show and is described as essentially perfect, followed by two electric sets that build energy; overall pacing is tight with a particularly uplifting third‑set finale.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Garcia praised for solid playing throughout, extra licks on Mexicali Blues, RxR Blues and one‑of‑a‑kind solos.
band_member_comments.Drums: Billy and Mickey highlighted for driving the groove on Music Never Stopped; drummers noted as energetic.
